content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Mickey Bennett                                Date : July 21, 1978

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim and Lee Rosenthal

Subject: Public Advocates as a source of income to
         Natalie West

The facts as they have been related to us, are as follows:
Natalie West, Litigation Counsel for the Fair Practices
Commission, is married to Peter Sly, who also is an attorney.
Recently, Mr. Sly was paid in excess of $500 in return for
rendering legal services to Public Advocates. Since Ms. West
has a community property interest in the fees paid to her
husband, Public Advocates is a source of income to her within
the meaning of Government Code Sections 87103 and 82030.1/

At present, the Commission is in litigation with Consumers
Union, which is represented by Public Advocates. The litigation
concerns the validity of a regulation adopted by the Commission.
Consumers Union was victorious at the Superior Court level and
the trial court judge ruled that Public Advocates was entitled
to attorney's fees. They have applied to the court for an award
of $150,000. Subsequently, an appellate court reversed the
Superior Court decision. However, we anticipate that Public
Advocates will seek a rehearing and will also such a hearing in
the Supreme Court if they are unsuccessful in changing the Court
of Appeal decision.

The question is whether Ms. West may participate in the
preparation of the Commission's legal papers in the litigation
in the event that Public Advocates further pursues the litigation
on behalf of its client.

Section 87100 provides that a public official may not make,
participate in the making or use his official position to influence
a governmental decision which will foreseeably and materially affect
her source of income. In our opinion, Ms. West would be making

1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise noted.

Mickey Bennett
July 21, 1979
Page Two

and participating in the making of a governmental decision if
she were to prepare the Commission's response to a petition for
rehearing or a petition for bearing in the Supreme Court. More-
over, in participating in the court case, Ms. West is using her
official position to influence a government decision - the
decision of the court as to the legality of the Commission's
regulation.

It is also foreseeable that the Commission's and the court's
actions will materially affect Public Advocates, a source of in-
come to Ms. West. Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
1802 provides that an increase or decrease of $50,000 or more
in the revenues received by a source of income is generally
presumed to be material. Therefore, since Public Advocates stands
to lose more than $50,000 in attorneys' fees if they do not pre-
vail in the litigation, it is reasonably foreseeable that the
effect of the decision will be material to Public Advocates.

Accordingly, it is our conclusion that Ms. West should
disqualify herself from any further participation in the Consumers
Union case.

In closing, we wish to add that from our long association
with Ms. West, we have no doubts that, in fact, she would not
be biased in any way by virtue of the fees paid by Public Advocates
to her husband. However, disqualification under the Political
Reform Act does not turn on our subjective appraisal of Ms. West's
loyalty; rather, it is based upon objective facts which suggest
the possibility that an official's financial interests could
interfere with her impartial decision making. Accordingly, we
have concluded that the Political Reform Act requires that
Ms. West refrain from any further participation in the Consumers
Union litigation.


TP:LR:plh

CITIZENS TO PROTECT POINT CONCEPTION
WHO is a Nonprofit Corporation
311 East Cannon Perdido
Santa Barbara, California 93101
(805) 965-5473

Branch Office:
592 West Sixth Street
Suite 720
Los Angeles
California 90014

May 23, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Advisory Opinion on Filing Responsibilities Pursuant to Government
    Code Section 86108(b)

Dear Commissioners:

    Attached hereto please find a copy of a newspaper
advertisement published in many state newspapers on May 16,
1978. It requests California citizens to contact the Governor
to assure that all relevant information is made public to
the California Coastal Commission and the California Public
Utilities Commission before each makes its decision as
required by Senate Bill 1041.

    As provided by Government Code Section 3114, we
respectfully ask your opinion or advice on whether the
expenditures for this advertisement should be reported by
Citizens to Protect Point Conception, under Government Code
Section 85103, especially in light of your Regulation 1420*.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this
matter.

                                    Sincerely yours,

                                    [signature]
                                    Chase Morgan
                                    President
